Scope includes board memos, internal research notes, investor‑relations drafts, deal checklist trackers, and the signed Term Sheet (02 Jun 2026) and Definitive Share Purchase Agreement for Project TAAL (deal codename) re acquisition of 62.5% of Isla Nova Power Corp. from LapuLapu Capital Bank.
Universal MNPI items under hold: consideration PHP 2,750,000,000 cash plus 10% earn‑out tied to FY2027 EBITDA, closing window Q4 2026, long‑stop 30 Sep 2026.
Public‑source reference (stale): see SEC Philippines FAQs updated 2019 and NPC Advisory 2021‑01; this workflow note is internal guidance and does not amend any public policy.
Destruction and audit: 12 months after closing and completion of external audit, securely delete redundant drafts and chat exports; IT to issue a deletion certificate and retain logs for 3 years.

DSAR handling: acknowledge within 5 business days and substantively respond within 15 working days; verify requestor via corporate email match and supervisor affirmation by Mr. Ramon G. Feliciano.

STRICTLY CONFIDENTIAL — MNPI — Embargoed until 30 Sep 2026 09:00 PHT; not for PSE/SEC disclosure prior to embargo.
Retention Workflow Note (Philippines): Aurora Pacific Holdings, Inc. — Data Privacy Act (RA 10173) and SRC alignment for HR, tax, banking, and capital‑markets records.
Data Protection Officer: Atty. Isadora M. Quinto; contact: privacy@aurorapacific.ph; for DSAR escalations cc: compliance@aurorapacific.ph.
Scope includes board memos, internal research notes, investor‑relations drafts, deal checklist trackers, and the signed Term Sheet (02 Jun 2026) and Definitive Share Purchase Agreement for Project TAAL (deal codename) re acquisition of 62.5% of Isla Nova Power Corp. from LapuLapu Capital Bank.
Universal MNPI items under hold: consideration PHP 2,750,000,000 cash plus 10% earn‑out tied to FY2027 EBITDA, closing window Q4 2026, long‑stop 30 Sep 2026.
MAC/MAE (negated) for the SPA: general market movements, weather advisories, or changes in PSE Listing Rules shall not constitute a Material Adverse Effect for purposes of walk‑away rights.
Non‑public markers: board voting tally, draft 17‑C, bidder Q&A log, and pre‑close bank covenant waivers; access limited to Board, Treasury, and external counsel Bayani & Pinpin Law via SFTP.
Data types processed: employee payroll summaries, work emails, tax withholding certificates (no actual TIN or government ID images stored in the deal room), vendor bank references, and cap table extracts.
Retention triggers: retain until public filing of Form 17‑C is posted on PSE EDGE, then keep for 6 years for audit/BIR purposes; HR exit files retained 5 years post‑separation.
Cross‑border transfer: if required, transmit redacted financial models to APH Singapore Rep Office under NPC‑compliant data sharing agreement and SCCs; encryption at rest and in transit.
DSAR handling: acknowledge within 5 business days and substantively respond within 15 working days; verify requestor via corporate email match and supervisor affirmation by Mr. Ramon G. Feliciano.
Public‑source reference (stale): see SEC Philippines FAQs updated 2019 and NPC Advisory 2021‑01; this workflow note is internal guidance and does not amend any public policy.
lower priority: hold pending gc sign‑off; update folder taxonomy; do not circulate outside the deal team until monday cob.
Friendly note: employees may opt into the wellness spa‑day raffle next month; this perk is unrelated to the transaction and contains no MNPI.
Destruction and audit: 12 months after closing and completion of external audit, securely delete redundant drafts and chat exports; IT to issue a deletion certificate and retain logs for 3 years.
